description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a modern yet traditional design, featuring a mix of horizontal and vertical white siding and a prominent shingled gable roof over the garage. The front facade is characterized by large, multi-pane windows and a central entryway, while the rear and side elevations reveal a combination of bay windows and flat roof sections. The upper floor includes open rooms visible from above, with interior walls painted in pastel colors and a blue-tiled bathroom. The house has a built-in garage on the left side, and the main entrance is centrally located beneath a small overhang. The window arrangement is mostly symmetrical, with a distinctive bay window section at the rear. The overall structure is boxy but softened by the use of traditional window grids and shingle textures.
description: A two-story suburban house with white siding, large multi-pane windows, a shingled gable roof over the garage, and a central entryway. The design blends modern and traditional elements with symmetrical window arrangements and visible pastel interiors.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, large multi-pane windows, and the shingled gable roof over the garage.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting the garage, side windows, and partial view of the open upper floor rooms.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the prominent bay windows, flat roof sections, and pastel-colored upper rooms.
description_view_3: Right-side view with vertical siding, side windows, and a clear look at the flat rear wall and upper floor layout.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with a single door, small overhang, and flanked by large multi-pane windows
